title: Salesforce Multi-Touch Attribution and Revenue Reporting
summary: Designed and implemented a weighted Salesforce attribution system connecting recent email, event, sales and organisational engagement with enquiries, referrals, opportunities, placements and revenue reporting.
details: Built separate Salesforce Flow entry paths for Lead enquiries, Contact enquiries and Account-level referrals. The model used a three-week engagement window, Campaign Member activity, recognised organisational domains, MAT and Account context, returning-customer status and sequence-sensitive weighting. It normalised applicable channel influence to 100%, stored the result in a custom Attribution Model object and supplied structured data for dashboards and revenue reports.

title: Local-Authority Account Hierarchy, Contract Pricing and Commercial Risk
summary: Designed a local-authority-centred Salesforce account model linking organisations, people, contracts, pricing and commercial exposure.
details: Represented local authorities as commercially relevant Accounts and connected their context across related Accounts, Leads and Contacts. Custom Metadata Type-driven assignment, Salesforce Flow and validation rules maintained the hierarchy, while Contract-object data and Opportunity automation supported relevant pricing. The resulting structure improved segmentation, reporting and visibility of authority-level commercial risk.
featuredMetric: Applied across 1,000+ Accounts and 20,000+ Leads and Contacts, representing approximately 240 local authorities.

title: MAT and Private Governing-Organisation Commercial Architecture
summary: Modelled schools within their Multi-Academy Trust, independent-school-group and wider governing-organisation relationships, then operationalised those relationships across Salesforce and MCAE.
details: Mapped school Accounts to MATs and other private governing organisations, represented those relationships through additional parent structures and created custom lookup keys so unconverted Leads could retain organisational context. A dynamic multi-object Screen Flow, validation rules and Custom Metadata Type-driven exact and wildcard domain matching maintained the model. Purchase-triggered Salesforce Campaigns then used related buying activity as a signal for MCAE validation, grading, nurture and sales routing.
featuredMetric: Mapped approximately 50% of school Accounts to 215 MATs and private governing organisations.

title: Salesforce Lead Opportunity Forecasting and Automated Closure
summary: Built a pre-conversion Lead opportunity process that guided sales activity, calculated forecast value, preserved repeated attempts and closed inactive work automatically.
details: Created a guided Salesforce Screen Flow with four dependent progression stages covering identification, outreach, response and outcome. Forecast value used the previous 12 months of purchasing data, including organisation type, rate, weekly hours, service duration and MAT context. A dedicated custom object preserved every opportunity attempt while the Lead displayed only the current cycle, and scheduled automation closed inactive opportunities after 21 days while retaining user-recorded and system-generated outcomes for reporting.
featuredMetric: Used 12 months of purchasing data and automatically closed inactive opportunities after 21 days.

title: Salesforce Access Governance, Data Standards and User Adoption
summary: Governed departmental access to Salesforce objects, fields and Flows while standardising core CRM data and supporting users through testing, training and troubleshooting.
details: Served as the departmental Salesforce super user and configured permission sets, object permissions, field-level access and validation controls for custom functionality. Standardised Lead, Contact and Account data, tested changes across different access levels, resolved user issues and translated technical changes into practical documentation, training and coaching. The work balanced usability with data protection and process integrity without implying organisation-wide platform ownership.
featuredMetric: Created a controlled departmental model for access, data quality and adoption.

title: Commercial Systems Operating Model and Change Leadership
summary: Aligned sales, marketing, CRM and operational teams around clearer ownership, repeatable workflows, governance and shared commercial information.
details: Gathered requirements across technical, commercial and marketing stakeholders, identified duplication and unclear ownership, and redesigned cross-functional workflows with defined responsibilities, checkpoints and escalation routes. Supported implementation through senior-stakeholder communication, documentation, training, coaching and sustained adoption. This work connected platform change with practical operating practices rather than treating Salesforce and MCAE as isolated technical systems.
featuredMetric: Established shared operating practices across sales, marketing, CRM and operations.

title: Mailchimp-to-MCAE Migration, Data Remediation and Platform Rebuild
summary: Led the transition from a high-risk Mailchimp and Pro-Net environment into a governed MCAE instance built largely from scratch.
details: Reviewed legacy records, duplication, consent risks and unusable data before rationalising the database and moving viable prospects into MCAE. Rebuilt the marketing-automation foundation around Salesforce integration, controlled audience management, consent and suppression rules, preference handling, send hygiene and repeatable campaign processes. The transition replaced fragmented practices with a more governed operating environment.
featuredMetric: Reduced approximately 30,000 legacy contacts to 19,000 viable prospects and improved successful-send rate to approximately 99%.

title: Self-Healing MCAE Audience, Consent and Buyer-Qualification Architecture
summary: Designed a self-healing audience and qualification system covering suppression, preferences, remediation, segmentation, scoring, grading and long-cycle nurture.
details: Built an MCAE architecture of approximately 400 lists using dynamic lists for current states and static lists for historical actions. A dynamic Master Suppression List, preference and unsubscribe controls, missing-data remediation and merge-tag protection governed sendability. Three core commercial audiences, behavioural scoring, profile grading and audience-prioritisation logic supported long-cycle nurture journeys, while campaign checks and release controls reduced operational risk.
featuredMetric: Built approximately 400 MCAE lists within a governed, permission-aware audience architecture.

title: MCAE Campaign Operations, Performance and Revenue Influence
summary: Operated MCAE as a segmented campaign and performance channel across email, events, webinars and gated content, connecting activity with commercial influence.
details: Planned, built and measured more than 50 segmented campaigns across email, events, webinars and gated content. Audience selection, Salesforce visibility, testing and stakeholder reporting were combined with broader performance measures covering delivery, engagement, progression and downstream commercial influence. The operating model increased average open rates and created evidence of email-attributed and email-influenced revenue without conflating campaign execution with the separate attribution model.
featuredMetric: Managed 50+ campaigns, increased average open rate from approximately 8% to 40%, achieved an 84% best campaign open rate and influenced approximately £419,475 in revenue.

title: Modular MCAE XHTML Email Template System
summary: Designed and delivered MCAE's standard modular email-production framework after the Mailchimp migration removed the previous drag-and-drop content-block workflow.
details: Analysed commercially successful Mailchimp layouts, translated recurring patterns into reusable modules and applied a consistent brand system across MCAE templates. Used AI-assisted XHTML generation alongside manual code review, Gmail and Outlook testing, security-conscious structures, documentation and user training. The framework was expanded incrementally as campaign requirements developed and became the standard production approach for recurring emails.
featuredMetric: Created approximately 13 reusable modules across 13 templates, supporting up to 48 combinations and reducing typical production from several days to approximately one day.

title: AI-Ready Salesforce, Pardot and Website-Automation Architecture
summary: Built governed Salesforce and Pardot/MCAE data, Campaign, segmentation and website-handoff foundations designed to support future AI-assisted workflows.
details: Created structured Lead and Contact data models, Salesforce Campaign connections, journey-eligibility rules and segmentation pathways intended to provide future agents with governed commercial context. Zapier connected website activity and form submissions with Pardot/MCAE automations. A future agentic attribution enhancement was also designed, but no production AI model, agent or AI-driven attribution layer was deployed after funding for the required AI tokens was withdrawn.
featuredMetric: Completed the AI-ready data and automation foundations; the AI execution layer was not deployed.

title: Local-Authority Commercial Intelligence Application
summary: Built a custom commercial-intelligence application that brought local-authority, Account and Bid information into a more usable decision-support view.
details: Defined the requirements, data relationships and user workflow for a Salesforce application that consolidated authority, Account and Bid information. Used AI-assisted Apex and Salesforce component development with manual testing, validation and configuration to create a decision-support layer on top of the local-authority data model. The application improved access to commercial context while keeping the underlying hierarchy and pricing architecture as a separate project.
featuredMetric: Covered approximately 200 local authorities, 1,000+ Accounts and around 2,500 annual Bids.

title: AI-Assisted Newsletter Engagement and Privacy-Conscious Leaderboard
summary: Designed, built, tested and deployed a web application concept that turned newsletter engagement into a visible, privacy-conscious participation experience.
details: Translated an engagement concept into a stakeholder-facing web application using structured prompting, iterative testing, debugging and interface refinement. The product incorporated privacy-conscious display names, ranking logic, consent and data-minimisation considerations, with GitHub used for version control and Vercel for deployment. The application was built and published, although the associated public newsletter launch was delayed.
featuredMetric: Built and published a functioning web application; the public newsletter launch was delayed.

title: Webinar and Event Registration, CRM and Follow-Up Automation
summary: Connected event and webinar promotion, registration, attendance and follow-up with Salesforce Campaigns and MCAE audience journeys.
details: Structured the lifecycle from invitation and registration through attendance capture, Salesforce Campaign membership, CRM visibility and segmented MCAE follow-up. The workflow supported event-influence reporting and allowed participation to contribute to wider customer journeys. Delivery included email activity for events and webinars as well as live Zoom operation or hosting, without implying responsibility as the content presenter.
featuredMetric: Supported email activity for 8 events and 2 webinars, including invitations, promotion and live Zoom operation or hosting.

title: Youth Programme Revitalisation, Audience Growth and Cost Control
summary: Revitalised youth events and community programmes through stronger programming, grassroots marketing, partnership development and operational control.
details: Reviewed the programme portfolio, improved the relevance and consistency of events, strengthened grassroots promotion and built relationships with participants, families and partner organisations. Combined audience development with team leadership, operational planning and budget control to improve participation and delivery quality. The programme created sustained growth rather than treating each event as an isolated activity.
featuredMetric: Tripled average attendance, doubled the client base and reduced departmental spending by 30% while maintaining delivery quality.

title: Large-Scale International Conference Operations and Volunteer Leadership
summary: Supported the planning and live delivery of large, multi-day international conferences across registration, access, volunteers, participant services, VIP operations and risk controls.
details: Coordinated practical workstreams involving registration, access systems, volunteer briefing and allocation, participant movement, VIP support, communications and contingency responses. The work required detailed preparation, rapid live problem-solving and close coordination across teams while maintaining participant experience and operational control throughout multi-day international events.
featuredMetric: Supported major conferences attended by more than 30,000 people.
